rulings.Case Title: DAVID ROBERTS VS. ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default Against Associated Insulation Of California, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 7, 2016, line 1. INTERVENOR FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default Against Associated Insulation Of California, Inc. Intervenor Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion to set aside entry of default against Associated Insulation of California, Inc. is granted. The March 12, 2012 and March 4, 2013 entries of default against Associated Insulation of California, Inc. are set aside. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN GRANADOS ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication, Along With Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 7, 2016, line 2. DEFENDANT FREDERICK MEISWINKEL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication. Defendant Frederick Meiswinkel, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs' inability to identify specific jobsites during specific years during which Mr. Granados worked in the presence of defendant's employees, and plaintiffs' inability to identify a brand/manufacturer of joint compound with which they worked, necessarily requires a conclusion that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Granados was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN GRANADOS ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 7, 2016, line 2. DEFENDANT FDCC CALIFORNIA, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant FDCC California, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant bases its arguments on the proposition that the two PG&E sites on Beale Street are the only jobsites where Mr. Granados worked where Dinwiddie employees were present and the deposition testimony and discovery responses demonstrate that plaintiffs do not have evidence to show that Dinwiddie employees exposed him to asbestos at those two sites. Defendant failed to demonstrate, though, that the two PG&E sites are the only sites throughout his career at which Mr. Granados worked where Dinwiddie employees were present. As to defendant's argument related to plaintiffs' response separate statement, the Court will consider plaintiffs' opposition papers. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/GLW)
